questions: journal paper ar conference paper er difference ki?
answers: A research paper is a scholarly document presenting original research findings, analysis, and conclusions. It typically includes abstract, introduction, literature review, methodology, results, discussion, and references.
Research paper likhte hole first topic select koro, literature review koro, research gap identify koro, methodology design koro, data collect ar analyze koro, then findings present koro.

Research papers follow a structured format: Abstract, Introduction, Literature Review, Methodology, Results, Discussion, Conclusion, and References. Each section has a specific purpose in presenting your research.

To publish a research paper, choose a suitable journal or conference, follow their formatting guidelines, submit your manuscript, respond to peer review feedback, and revise accordingly. The process can take months.
